title: B1 Listening - Part 1 - Test 2
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear two students talking about a music lesson.
items.audio_script: Boy: Are we still meeting in the main hall for the music lesson? I wrote it down yesterday, but I cannot find the message now. || Girl: That was the original plan, but a drama group is using the hall this afternoon. [1]We need to meet in the small room next to the office instead[/1]. || Boy: Thanks for telling me. I would have gone to the wrong place and wondered where everyone was. || Girl: No problem. I will arrive ten minutes early, so look for me near the entrance. Keep your phone on in case anything changes again.
items.question: Where is the music lesson today?

options.A: In the small room next to the office

items.explanation: The lesson is in the small room next to the office.

items.context: You hear two friends preparing to make a cake.
items.audio_script: Girl: Are you ready for making the cake after school yet? I put some butter in my bag because I thought that was what we needed. || Boy: That was on the first list, but there is already plenty of butter in the fridge. [1]Everyone needs to bring six eggs now[/1]. || Girl: Good thing I asked. I was about to leave my bag by the door and forget about it. || Boy: You still have time. Put it in now, and check the final message once more before we leave tomorrow morning.
items.question: What do they need to buy?

items.context: You hear two family members talking about after school.
items.audio_script: Boy: Are we still studying maths together after school today? I told my sister I would be home later, so I need to know. || Girl: That was the idea, but Dad needs it clean before he drives to work tomorrow. [1]We are going to help Dad wash the car instead[/1]. || Boy: That actually sounds better. I nearly paid for the other activity online this morning. || Girl: It is a good thing you had not booked anything yet. I will book our places this evening and send you the time. We can meet at the bus stop after lunch.
items.question: What will the speaker do after school?
items.explanation: The speaker will help wash the car.

items.context: You hear two students talking about the bus home.
items.audio_script: Girl: I need to write down the time for the bus home. Is it still at four o'clock, like you said yesterday? || Boy: No, the driver has to collect another class first. [1]It starts at twenty past four now[/1], although we should arrive ten minutes earlier. || Girl: That is useful to know. I would have been waiting there at the wrong time. || Boy: I will send you a reminder later. Bring everything you need, because there will not be time to go home again before it begins.
items.question: What time does the bus leave?
items.explanation: The bus now leaves at twenty past four.

items.context: You hear two people talking about a hotel room.
items.audio_script: Woman: How are you getting on with your hotel room? I heard it was not working properly. || Man: I first thought the street outside was too noisy, and my brother wondered if the bed was uncomfortable. But he checked it carefully last night. [1]The shower does not work at all[/1]. || Woman: Can it be fixed before the tour tomorrow morning? || Man: Yes, probably. We know what is wrong now, and the repair shop said it should be ready tomorrow afternoon. That is a relief because I need it this weekend.
items.question: What is wrong with the hotel room?
items.explanation: The problem is the broken shower.

items.context: You hear two people discussing a journey to the airport.
items.audio_script: Man: Are you still travelling to the airport by car? I could meet you when you arrive. || Woman: I planned to, but parking there costs far too much. I considered going by underground, but there are too many bags to carry downstairs. [1]I am going by coach instead[/1]. || Man: Good idea. What time will you get there? || Woman: Just before eleven, if everything is on time. I have already checked the timetable online, so it should be easy. I will message you when I leave, so you do not have to wait outside.
items.question: How will they go to the airport?
items.explanation: They decide to take the coach.

items.context: You hear two friends talking about lunch.
items.audio_script: Girl: Did you buy a sandwich for lunch? I saw one in the shop window yesterday and thought it looked nice. || Boy: No. I looked at a sandwich, and I nearly chose a slice of pizza, but the sandwiches had sold out and the pizza looked cold. [1]I bought a salad in the end[/1]. || Girl: Good choice. It sounds more useful, and I think it will be appreciated. || Boy: I hope so. I kept the receipt, just in case it needs changing after we give it.
items.question: What did the speaker buy for lunch?
